reason: INLINE-SUBMULTIPLICATIVITY VERDICT: GT-opnorm-submult byte-grounds the operator-norm DEFINITION ||w||=sup_{||Z||<=1}||w(Z)|| and the phrase 'bounded linear operators form a Banach algebra' (both byte-verified at refs/kitaev-2405.02434/approximate_algebras.tex:640 and :642). It does NOT byte-ground the literal inequality ||uw||<=||u||*||w||. The external derives submultiplicativity inline from the sup-definition: for ||Z||<=1, ||(uw)(Z)||=||u(w(Z))||<=||u||*||w(Z)||<=||u||*||w||*||Z||<=||u||*||w||, then sup over Z. VERDICT: the inline derivation is mathematically VALID and self-contained from the byte-grounded sup-definition (it is the textbook two-line proof that the operator norm is submultiplicative), so it SUFFICES as a grounded leaf under the project's grounded-leaf-or-proved rule. CAVEAT for accretion: 'bounded operators form a Banach algebra' is asserted by Kitaev for the eps-Banach setting; the eps=0 specialization (ordinary B(H), exact associativity) used here is the standard case and the inline sup-argument does not even need the Banach-algebra clause. RECOMMENDATION (non-blocking): when the matrix-analysis source stating ||AB||<=||A||*||B|| directly is acquired, add it as a second grounding locus; meanwhile the inline derivation is acceptable. No defect found.
